statement.en: Red List of birds in Slovakia: update 2013–2023
statement.summary: The national Red List was updated for the period 2013–2023, based on the improved knowledge and the change in the conservation status of some rare bird species. IUCN categories and criteria according to version 3.1 were used and recommendations for their application according to version 15.1. Version 4.0 of the IUCN recommendations was taken into account for the application of the criteria at the national and regional levels. Altogether 213 native breeding species were assessed (of the total number of 233 species of breeding birds). A total 94 of 213 native species met the criteria for inclusion in the Red List (five species RE – regionally extinct, 12 CR – critically endangered, 24 EN – endangered, 29 VU – vulnerable, and 24 NT – near threatened), which represents up to 40% of the species

proposer: Miroslav DEMKO, Peter Lešo, Jozef RIDZOŇ et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: This is an empirical ornithological survey with established methodology (IUCN Red List v3.1, v15.1, v4.0 criteria). The assessment is based on verifiable observation, population data, and standardized classification protocol. The findings are factual documentation of conservation status at a specific temporal-geographic scope (Slovakia, 2013–2023).

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: スロバキアの鳥類の5分の2が、絶滅の崖縁に立つ。
